creator: mike@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Sat, Jul 21, 2001 at 06:15:33PM -0400, Rob Austein wrote:
>    Date: Sat, 21 Jul 2001 11:36:51 -0700 (PDT)
>    From: <mike@FreeBSD.org>
> 
>    Synopsis: FreeBSD 3.3 sends ICMP reply to IP unicast received as ethernet broadcast
> 
>    State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
>    State-Changed-By: mike
>    State-Changed-When: Sat Jul 21 11:36:37 PDT 2001
>    State-Changed-Why: 
> 
>    Does this problem still occur in newer versions of FreeBSD,
>    such as 4.3-RELEASE?
> 
>    http://www.FreeBSD.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=16013
> 
> Dunno.  The particular misconfigured production network that triggered
> the bad reply no longer exists.  I'll see if I can reproduce the
> problem in the lab when I get a chance and get back to you on that.

Thanks.

Best regards,
Mike Barcroft
